$comment: Chrono History deck. 100 cards. 'have:true' = image already generated. 'description' is the guess clue shown to players (never contains the year; short and clear, names allowed). 'scene' drives image generation. 'year' is numeric (negative = BCE) for sorting/scoring; 'yearLabel' is shown on reveal.
style: grand neoclassical oil painting, vertical portrait composition, dramatic chiaroscuro lighting, rich earthy palette, in the style of the old masters, oil on canvas, visible brushstrokes, aged varnish, museum masterpiece

cards.title: Unification of Egypt
cards.description: King Narmer unites Upper and Lower Egypt under the first pharaoh.
cards.scene: the first pharaoh uniting Upper and Lower Egypt, a king in the double crown receiving tribute beside the Nile, ancient Egyptian temple and hieroglyphs

cards.title: Great Pyramid
cards.description: Egyptians complete the Great Pyramid of Giza for Pharaoh Khufu.
cards.scene: the building of the Great Pyramid of Giza, thousands of laborers hauling massive limestone blocks up ramps under a blazing sun, the pyramid rising over the desert

cards.title: Code of Hammurabi
cards.description: King Hammurabi of Babylon carves his famous law code into stone.
cards.scene: a Babylonian king receiving laws from a sun god, the tall black stone stele of Hammurabi inscribed with cuneiform in a Mesopotamian temple

cards.title: Battle of Kadesh
cards.description: Egypt and the Hittites fight the Battle of Kadesh, the largest chariot battle in history.
cards.scene: the Battle of Kadesh, Egyptian and Hittite war chariots clashing in a vast dusty plain, a pharaoh firing arrows from his golden chariot

cards.title: First Olympic Games
cards.description: The first Olympic Games are held at Olympia in ancient Greece.
cards.scene: the first ancient Olympic Games, nude Greek athletes racing and wrestling before a crowd at Olympia, a temple and olive wreaths

cards.title: Founding of Rome
cards.description: Romulus founds the city of Rome.
cards.scene: the legendary founding of Rome, the twins Romulus and Remus suckled by a she-wolf, a founder marking out city walls on a hill with the Tiber below

cards.title: Birth of the Buddha
cards.description: Siddhartha Gautama, the Buddha, is born in northern India.
cards.scene: the enlightenment of the Buddha, a serene prince meditating beneath a great bodhi fig tree, soft golden light, ancient India

cards.title: Birth of Confucius
cards.description: The philosopher Confucius is born in China.
cards.scene: the Chinese philosopher Confucius teaching disciples in a garden of ancient China, scholar robes, scrolls, calm wise sage

cards.title: Roman Republic
cards.description: Romans overthrow their last king and found the Roman Republic.
cards.scene: the founding of the Roman Republic, Roman senators and patricians swearing an oath over a sword in the forum, the tyrant king driven out

cards.title: Battle of Marathon
cards.description: Athens defeats the invading Persians at the Battle of Marathon.
cards.scene: the Battle of Marathon, Greek hoplites in bronze armor and crested helmets charging the Persian army on a coastal plain, shields and spears

cards.title: Thermopylae
cards.description: King Leonidas and 300 Spartans make their last stand against Persia at Thermopylae.
cards.scene: the last stand at Thermopylae, three hundred Spartan warriors with red cloaks and round shields holding a narrow mountain pass against a vast Persian army

cards.title: The Parthenon
cards.description: Athens begins building the Parthenon on the Acropolis.
cards.scene: the construction of the Parthenon on the Acropolis, Greek workers raising marble columns of a great temple above Athens, sculptors at work

cards.title: Death of Socrates
cards.description: The philosopher Socrates is executed in Athens, made to drink hemlock.
cards.scene: the death of Socrates, the philosopher calmly reaching for a cup of hemlock surrounded by grieving disciples in an Athenian prison cell, in the style of Jacques-Louis David

cards.title: Alexander the Great
cards.description: Alexander the Great becomes king of Macedon.
cards.scene: Alexander the Great as a young king on his rearing warhorse Bucephalus, leading the Macedonian army, banners and spears, classical antiquity

cards.title: Qin Unifies China
cards.description: Qin Shi Huang unifies China as its first emperor.
cards.scene: Qin Shi Huang the first emperor of China enthroned in imperial robes, ranks of terracotta soldiers and the beginnings of the Great Wall behind him

cards.title: Hannibal Crosses the Alps
cards.description: Hannibal crosses the Alps with war elephants to attack Rome.
cards.scene: Hannibal leading his army and war elephants across the snowy Alps, soldiers struggling through mountain passes in a blizzard, dramatic peaks

cards.title: Death of Caesar
cards.description: Roman senators assassinate Julius Caesar on the Ides of March.
cards.scene: the assassination of Julius Caesar in the Roman Senate, toga-clad senators with daggers surrounding the falling Caesar at the base of a marble statue

cards.title: Birth of Jesus
cards.description: Jesus of Nazareth is born in Bethlehem.
cards.scene: the nativity, a newborn child in a humble stable with his mother, shepherds and wise men kneeling, a bright star above, warm candlelight

cards.title: The Crucifixion
cards.description: Jesus of Nazareth is crucified in Jerusalem.
cards.scene: the crucifixion on a hill outside Jerusalem, three crosses against a dark stormy sky, mourners gathered below, dramatic shafts of light

cards.title: Vesuvius
cards.description: Mount Vesuvius erupts and buries Pompeii and Herculaneum.
cards.scene: the eruption of Mount Vesuvius destroying Pompeii, a towering black ash cloud and falling fire over Roman columns, citizens fleeing, red glow

cards.title: Hadrian's Wall
cards.description: Rome begins building Hadrian’s Wall across northern Britain.
cards.scene: the building of Hadrian's Wall across the misty hills of northern Britain, Roman legionaries and laborers raising a long stone wall under grey skies

cards.title: Constantine's Vision
cards.description: Constantine wins the Battle of the Milvian Bridge and embraces Christianity.
cards.scene: the Battle of the Milvian Bridge, Emperor Constantine on horseback beneath a glowing cross in the sky, Roman cavalry charging across a river bridge

cards.title: Visigoths Sack Rome
cards.description: The Visigoths under Alaric sack the city of Rome.
cards.scene: the Visigoths sacking Rome, fur-clad barbarian warriors looting and burning marble buildings, fleeing citizens, columns ablaze at dusk

cards.title: Fall of Rome
cards.description: Odoacer deposes the last Western Roman emperor; the Western Empire falls.
cards.scene: the fall of the Western Roman Empire, a young boy-emperor surrendering his golden crown to a fur-clad Germanic chieftain in a decaying Roman hall

cards.title: The Hijra
cards.description: Muhammad flees Mecca for Medina, beginning the Islamic calendar.
cards.scene: the Hijra, the prophet Muhammad and his companion traveling by camel across the Arabian desert at night toward Medina, palm trees and stars

cards.title: Charlemagne Crowned
cards.description: The pope crowns Charlemagne the first Holy Roman Emperor.
cards.scene: the coronation of Charlemagne, the pope placing a golden crown on the kneeling Frankish king in a great cathedral, candlelight and rich robes

cards.title: The Tale of Genji
cards.description: Murasaki Shikibu writes The Tale of Genji in Japan, often called the first novel.
cards.scene: a Japanese noblewoman of the Heian court writing The Tale of Genji with brush and ink on scrolls, silk kimono, a refined palace garden, autumn maples

cards.title: Hastings
cards.description: William the Conqueror defeats King Harold at the Battle of Hastings.
cards.scene: the Battle of Hastings, William the Conqueror on horseback leading Norman knights with lances against the Anglo-Saxon shield wall, banners and arrows

cards.title: First Crusade
cards.description: Pope Urban II launches the First Crusade to retake Jerusalem.
cards.scene: Pope Urban II preaching the First Crusade to a crowd of armored knights and nobles, banners with red crosses, a medieval council outdoors

cards.title: Genghis Khan
cards.description: Genghis Khan unites the Mongol tribes and founds his empire.
cards.scene: Genghis Khan proclaimed great khan of the Mongols on the steppe, a fierce nomad leader on horseback before mounted warriors and felt tents, banners

cards.title: Magna Carta
cards.description: English barons force King John to seal the Magna Carta.
cards.scene: the sealing of Magna Carta, an English king reluctantly affixing his seal to a charter surrounded by armored barons in a meadow at Runnymede

cards.title: Marco Polo
cards.description: Marco Polo sets out from Venice on his journey to China.
cards.scene: Marco Polo and merchants traveling a camel caravan along the Silk Road toward the East, mountains and deserts, distant Chinese palaces

cards.title: Founding of Tenochtitlan
cards.description: The Aztecs found their capital, Tenochtitlan, on a lake island.
cards.scene: the founding of Tenochtitlan, an eagle perched on a cactus over a lake island, Aztecs building a great temple city of canals and pyramids

cards.title: The Black Death
cards.description: The Black Death reaches Europe, killing a third of its people.
cards.scene: the Black Death in a medieval town, plague doctors in beaked masks and cloaked figures among the dying, carts of bodies, grey desolate streets

cards.title: Ming Dynasty
cards.description: The Ming dynasty is founded as the Mongols are driven from China.
cards.scene: the founding of the Ming dynasty, a new Chinese emperor enthroned in a golden palace, imperial dragon robes, courtiers bowing, red and gold

cards.title: Joan of Arc
cards.description: Joan of Arc is burned at the stake in France.
cards.scene: Joan of Arc burning at the stake, a young woman in armor tied to a post amid flames and smoke, soldiers and a crowd in a medieval square

cards.title: Fall of Constantinople
cards.description: The Ottomans capture Constantinople, ending the Byzantine Empire.
cards.scene: the fall of Constantinople, Ottoman soldiers storming the great walls under giant cannon fire, a sultan on horseback entering the city, the Hagia Sophia

cards.title: Gutenberg Bible
cards.description: Gutenberg prints the Bible using his movable-type printing press.
cards.scene: Johannes Gutenberg in his workshop examining the first printed Bible page beside his great wooden printing press, candlelight, ink and movable type

cards.title: Columbus
cards.description: Christopher Columbus reaches the Americas.
cards.scene: Columbus landing in the Americas, an explorer in Renaissance dress planting a flag on a tropical shore, sailing ships at anchor, curious natives watching

cards.title: Da Gama Reaches India
cards.description: Vasco da Gama reaches India by sea around Africa.
cards.scene: Vasco da Gama arriving by sailing ship at the coast of India, Portuguese sailors meeting Indian traders, spice market, palm-lined harbor

cards.title: The Mona Lisa
cards.description: Leonardo da Vinci begins painting the Mona Lisa.
cards.scene: Leonardo da Vinci in his Renaissance studio painting the Mona Lisa, an artist at an easel before a seated smiling woman, soft window light, Florence

cards.title: Luther's 95 Theses
cards.description: Martin Luther posts his 95 Theses, sparking the Reformation.
cards.scene: Martin Luther nailing his 95 Theses to the wooden door of a German church, a monk with hammer and paper, townsfolk gathering, autumn light

cards.title: Fall of the Aztecs
cards.description: Hernán Cortés and the Spanish conquer the Aztec Empire.
cards.scene: the fall of the Aztec Empire, Spanish conquistadors in steel armor with horses and cannon storming a great Aztec temple city, smoke over the lake

cards.title: Mughal Empire
cards.description: Babur wins at Panipat and founds the Mughal Empire in India.
cards.scene: Babur founding the Mughal Empire, a victorious emperor on a war elephant after the Battle of Panipat, cannon and cavalry, Indian palace banners

cards.title: Copernicus
cards.description: Copernicus publishes his theory that the Earth orbits the Sun.
cards.scene: Copernicus the astronomer studying the heavens, an old scholar with compass and celestial globe showing the sun at the center of the planets, candlelit study

cards.title: Birth of Shakespeare
cards.description: William Shakespeare is born in England.
cards.scene: William Shakespeare writing by candlelight with a quill, an Elizabethan playwright at a wooden desk with manuscripts, a globe theatre beyond the window

cards.title: Spanish Armada
cards.description: England defeats the Spanish Armada.
cards.scene: the defeat of the Spanish Armada, great galleons burning and scattering in a stormy sea, English fireships among them, dramatic clouds and waves

cards.title: Jamestown
cards.description: English settlers found Jamestown, their first colony in America.
cards.scene: the founding of Jamestown, English colonists in early 1600s dress building a wooden palisade fort in a wooded river clearing, ships on the water

cards.title: Galileo's Telescope
cards.description: Galileo discovers Jupiter’s moons with his telescope.
cards.scene: Galileo observing the night sky through his telescope from a rooftop, an astronomer pointing a long brass telescope at the moon and Jupiter, stars

cards.title: The Mayflower
cards.description: The Pilgrims land at Plymouth from the Mayflower.
cards.scene: the Pilgrims landing from the Mayflower at Plymouth, settlers in dark 1600s dress stepping onto a rocky winter shore, a sailing ship offshore, grey sky

cards.title: Taj Mahal
cards.description: Shah Jahan begins building the Taj Mahal for his late wife.
cards.scene: the building of the Taj Mahal, the gleaming white marble domed mausoleum rising by a river as thousands of laborers and elephants work, Mughal India

cards.title: Qing Dynasty
cards.description: The Manchu Qing dynasty seizes power in China.
cards.scene: the founding of the Qing dynasty, Manchu cavalry entering the gates of Beijing, a new emperor in dragon robes, banners over the Forbidden City

cards.title: Peace of Westphalia
cards.description: The Peace of Westphalia ends the Thirty Years’ War.
cards.scene: the signing of the Peace of Westphalia, richly dressed diplomats and nobles gathered around a table in a grand hall ratifying a treaty, 17th century

cards.title: Newton's Principia
cards.description: Isaac Newton publishes his laws of motion and gravity.
cards.scene: Isaac Newton in his study with the Principia, a scholar pondering gravity beneath an apple tree, prism and telescope, candlelight and manuscripts

cards.title: Lisbon Earthquake
cards.description: A massive earthquake, fire and tsunami destroy Lisbon.
cards.scene: the Great Lisbon earthquake, a baroque city collapsing in dust and flames as a tidal wave floods the harbor, people fleeing through ruined streets

cards.title: Declaration of Independence
cards.description: The American colonies declare independence from Britain.
cards.scene: the signing of the American Declaration of Independence, founders in 18th century dress gathered in a hall presenting a document, quill and flag

cards.title: Bastille
cards.description: Revolutionaries storm the Bastille, igniting the French Revolution.
cards.scene: the storming of the Bastille, a revolutionary crowd with muskets, pikes and tricolor cockades attacking the great stone fortress prison, cannon smoke over Paris

cards.title: Haitian Revolution
cards.description: Enslaved Haitians rise up against French rule, beginning their revolution.
cards.scene: the Haitian Revolution, formerly enslaved rebels with torches and weapons rising against colonial soldiers on a burning Caribbean sugar plantation at night

cards.title: Napoleon Emperor
cards.description: Napoleon crowns himself Emperor of France.
cards.scene: the coronation of Napoleon, the general crowning himself emperor in golden laurel and ermine robe before the pope in a cathedral, in the style of Jacques-Louis David

cards.title: Waterloo
cards.description: Napoleon is defeated for good at the Battle of Waterloo.
cards.scene: the Battle of Waterloo, Napoleonic infantry squares and cavalry charging through smoke on a muddy battlefield, cannon fire, fallen soldiers, grey sky

cards.title: First Steam Railway
cards.description: The world’s first public steam railway opens in England.
cards.scene: the first public steam railway, an early Stephenson locomotive billowing smoke pulling open carriages of cheering crowds past English countryside

cards.title: Queen Victoria
cards.description: Queen Victoria takes the British throne.
cards.scene: the young Queen Victoria ascending the throne, a youthful queen in coronation robes and crown receiving homage in a grand palace hall, Victorian splendor

cards.title: Communist Manifesto
cards.description: Marx and Engels publish The Communist Manifesto amid Europe’s revolutions.
cards.scene: the revolutions of 1848, workers and revolutionaries raising a red flag on a barricade in a European city street, smoke and cobblestones, pamphlets

cards.title: Origin of Species
cards.description: Charles Darwin publishes On the Origin of Species.
cards.scene: Charles Darwin the naturalist studying finches and specimens in his study, an old scholar with notebooks, a sailing ship and Galapagos creatures, Victorian

cards.title: American Civil War
cards.description: The American Civil War breaks out over slavery.
cards.scene: the American Civil War, ranks of Union and Confederate soldiers with rifles and cannon clashing through smoke on a battlefield, regimental flags

cards.title: Lincoln Assassinated
cards.description: President Abraham Lincoln is assassinated.
cards.scene: the assassination of Abraham Lincoln in a theater box, an assassin firing a pistol behind the seated president and his wife, gas-lit theatre, 1860s

cards.title: Suez Canal
cards.description: The Suez Canal opens, linking the Mediterranean and Red seas.
cards.scene: the opening of the Suez Canal, a procession of steamships sailing through a new desert canal lined with cheering crowds and flags, Egypt 1869

cards.title: The Telephone
cards.description: Alexander Graham Bell patents the telephone.
cards.scene: Alexander Graham Bell speaking into the first telephone, an inventor at a wooden apparatus with wires in a Victorian laboratory, excitement, gaslight

cards.title: The Light Bulb
cards.description: Thomas Edison invents a practical electric light bulb.
cards.scene: Edison and the electric light bulb, an inventor holding aloft a glowing glass bulb in a dim laboratory full of wires and apparatus, warm glow on faces

cards.title: First Automobile
cards.description: Karl Benz builds the first practical automobile.
cards.scene: Karl Benz with the first automobile, a man driving an early three-wheeled motor carriage down a cobbled German street as onlookers stare, 1880s

cards.title: Eiffel Tower
cards.description: The Eiffel Tower is completed in Paris.
cards.scene: the completion of the Eiffel Tower, the great iron lattice tower rising over Paris with crowds at a world fair below, balloons and flags, golden light

cards.title: First Film Screening
cards.description: The Lumière brothers hold the first public film screening.
cards.scene: the first public film screening by the Lumiere brothers, a small audience in a dim Paris cafe watching a flickering black and white moving image projected on a wall

cards.title: Transatlantic Radio
cards.description: Marconi sends the first radio signal across the Atlantic.
cards.scene: Marconi receiving the first transatlantic radio signal, an inventor with headphones and apparatus and a kite antenna on a windswept cliff above the ocean, 1901

cards.title: First Flight
cards.description: The Wright brothers make the first powered airplane flight at Kitty Hawk.
cards.scene: the Wright brothers' first powered airplane flight at Kitty Hawk, a fragile wooden biplane lifting off windswept sand dunes, one brother running alongside

cards.title: Special Relativity
cards.description: Albert Einstein publishes his theory of special relativity.
cards.scene: Einstein in his miracle year, a young thoughtful clerk at a desk covered in equations about light and time, a clock and beams of light, soft study

cards.title: Titanic
cards.description: The Titanic sinks after hitting an iceberg on its maiden voyage.
cards.scene: the sinking of the Titanic, a great ocean liner tilting and going down by the bow in a starlit icy sea, lifeboats and an iceberg, lights blazing

cards.title: World War I
cards.description: World War I breaks out across Europe.
cards.scene: the outbreak of World War One, soldiers in a muddy trench under barbed wire and shellfire, going over the top through smoke, grey desolate battlefield

cards.title: Russian Revolution
cards.description: The Bolsheviks seize power in the Russian Revolution.
cards.scene: the Russian Revolution, red-flag-waving workers and soldiers storming a palace in a snowy Petrograd square, rifles and banners, a fallen imperial eagle

cards.title: Tutankhamun's Tomb
cards.description: Howard Carter discovers the tomb of Tutankhamun in Egypt.
cards.scene: the discovery of Tutankhamun's tomb, an archaeologist by candlelight peering into a tomb chamber full of glittering golden treasure and a sarcophagus, Egypt

cards.title: Lindbergh's Flight
cards.description: Charles Lindbergh flies solo across the Atlantic.
cards.scene: Charles Lindbergh's solo transatlantic flight, a small silver monoplane flying alone over a vast moonlit ocean, then a cheering crowd at an airfield, 1920s

cards.title: Penicillin
cards.description: Alexander Fleming discovers penicillin.
cards.scene: Alexander Fleming discovering penicillin, a scientist in a lab coat examining a petri dish of mould under lamplight in a cluttered laboratory, 1920s

cards.title: Wall Street Crash
cards.description: The Wall Street Crash triggers the Great Depression.
cards.scene: the Wall Street Crash of 1929, panicked crowds of men in suits and hats outside a stock exchange, newspaper headlines, despair, a grey city canyon

cards.title: Hitler Becomes Chancellor
cards.description: Adolf Hitler becomes Chancellor of Germany.
cards.scene: 1933 Germany, a torchlit nighttime political rally with ranks of uniformed men and banners marching past a balcony, ominous searchlights, dark crowds

cards.title: World War II
cards.description: Nazi Germany invades Poland, starting World War II.
cards.scene: the outbreak of World War Two, columns of tanks and dive bombers advancing across a war-torn landscape, soldiers and refugees, smoke on the horizon

cards.title: End of WWII
cards.description: World War II ends after atomic bombs hit Hiroshima and Nagasaki.
cards.scene: the end of World War Two, a towering mushroom cloud rising over a ruined city, and soldiers and crowds celebrating victory with flags, 1945

cards.title: Indian Independence
cards.description: India wins independence from Britain and is partitioned.
cards.scene: Indian independence, jubilant crowds raising the new tricolor flag, a frail spinning-wheel leader honored, midnight celebration in 1947 Delhi

cards.title: People's Republic of China
cards.description: Mao Zedong proclaims the People’s Republic of China.
cards.scene: the founding of the People's Republic of China, a leader proclaiming the republic from a great red gate above a vast crowd with red flags, 1949 Beijing

cards.title: Structure of DNA
cards.description: Watson and Crick discover the double-helix structure of DNA.
cards.scene: the discovery of the DNA double helix, two scientists beside a tall metal molecular model of a twisting double helix in a 1950s laboratory, excitement

cards.title: Sputnik
cards.description: The Soviet Union launches Sputnik, the first satellite.
cards.scene: the launch of Sputnik, a rocket roaring into a night sky and a small silver sphere with antennas orbiting the earth among the stars, 1957

cards.title: First Human in Space
cards.description: Yuri Gagarin becomes the first human in space.
cards.scene: Yuri Gagarin first human in space, a smiling cosmonaut in a helmet inside a capsule orbiting the blue curve of the earth, a rocket trail below, 1961

cards.title: JFK Assassinated
cards.description: President John F. Kennedy is assassinated in Dallas.
cards.scene: the JFK assassination, a presidential open-top motorcade through a 1960s city plaza, crowds and a grassy hill, sudden shock, dark sedans and flags

cards.title: Moon Landing
cards.description: Apollo 11 lands the first humans on the Moon.
cards.scene: the Apollo 11 moon landing, an astronaut in a white spacesuit planting a flag on the grey lunar surface, the lunar module nearby and blue Earth in the black sky

cards.title: Apple Founded
cards.description: Steve Jobs and Steve Wozniak found Apple.
cards.scene: the founding of Apple, two young men assembling an early personal computer at a workbench in a 1970s suburban garage, circuit boards and tools, warm light

cards.title: Chernobyl
cards.description: The Chernobyl nuclear reactor explodes in Soviet Ukraine.
cards.scene: the Chernobyl disaster, a burning nuclear power plant reactor at night glowing under a sinister sky, an abandoned town with a ferris wheel, eerie green light

cards.title: Berlin Wall Falls
cards.description: The Berlin Wall falls, reuniting East and West.
cards.scene: the fall of the Berlin Wall, jubilant crowds atop the graffiti-covered concrete wall before the Brandenburg Gate, people with hammers and flags under floodlights at night

cards.title: World Wide Web
cards.description: Tim Berners-Lee invents the World Wide Web.
cards.scene: the invention of the World Wide Web, a scientist at a computer terminal in a 1990 office, glowing screen with linked pages and a global network of light, cables

cards.title: Soviet Union Dissolves
cards.description: The Soviet Union dissolves, ending the Cold War.
cards.scene: the dissolution of the Soviet Union, a red hammer-and-sickle flag being lowered over a snowy Kremlin at dusk, crowds in a Moscow square, 1991

cards.title: Mandela Elected
cards.description: Nelson Mandela is elected president as apartheid ends in South Africa.
cards.scene: Nelson Mandela elected president, a dignified leader raising a fist before a vast jubilant rainbow crowd voting, South African flags, sunrise, 1994

cards.title: Dolly the Sheep
cards.description: Scientists clone Dolly the sheep, the first cloned mammal.
cards.scene: Dolly the cloned sheep, a sheep standing in a laboratory pen as scientists in white coats observe, microscopes and beakers, a 1990s lab, soft light

cards.title: September 11
cards.description: Al-Qaeda hijackers destroy the World Trade Center towers in New York.
cards.scene: the September 11 attacks, two great smoking skyscraper towers against a clear blue morning sky over a city skyline, plumes of smoke and ash, somber
